title: centaurea alba subsp. leucomalla
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. leucomalla, also known as White Knapweed, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, inflammation, and various skin issues due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Centaurea alba subsp. leucomalla include its ability to reduce pain and swelling, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often used in traditional medicine to support skin health and treat various inflammatory conditions.
